notes: 10K-token CI fixture (pytest_large_run's bigger sibling). Same auth_expiry timezone bug, but buried in heavier startup logs, longer captured-log blocks per failure, ~700 tests across more modules, plus end-of-run flaky-history and stability-summary tail sections. The flaky-history section explicitly flags auth_expiry as a NEW (regression) failure, which is the diagnostic signal a human SRE would key on. B2 truncation at 200 tokens cuts off in the startup INFO block before progress; B3 deterministic summary preserves the WARNING lines but loses the regression context. B4 should locate the auth_expiry assertion span via search and produce a focused citation-backed diagnosis.
